sn43.44:1.1: “Monks, I will also teach you the ultimate and the path leading to the ultimate.
sn43.44:1.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.” “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One. The Blessed One said,
sn43.44:1.4: Whatever is the ending of passion, the ending of aversion, the ending of delusion:
sn43.44:1.6: “And which is the path leading to the ultimate?
sn43.44:1.8: This is called the path leading to the ultimate.
sn43.44:1.9: Thus, monks, I have taught you the ultimate and the path leading to the ultimate.
sn43.44:1.10: Whatever a sympathetic teacher should do—seeking the welfare of his disciples, out of sympathy for them—that have I done for you.
sn43.44:1.11: Over there are the roots of trees; over there, empty dwellings. Practice jhāna, monks. Don’t be heedless. Don’t later fall into remorse. This is our message to you all.”
